summary: ledgerflow-deploy-utils postinstall beacons AWS IMDS IAM role to hardcoded IP
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script queries the AWS instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 for `/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/` and then POSTs the IAM role name plus an IMDS-reachability flag over plain HTTP to a hardcoded remote IP (54.226.194.239/chain3). This auto-executes against any installer, reveals whether the host runs in AWS, and discloses which IAM role is attached — installer-side cloud identity reconnaissance with no relation to the package's stated 'deploy utils' purpose. The package itself is a near-empty stub (index.js exports only placeholder validate/deploy console.log calls) published to public npm under the name of what appears to be an internal LedgerFlow utility, matching the dependency-confusion lure shape: a thin public package whose only material behavior is the install-time beacon.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
